title: A Lantern Beside the Abbasid Oven
subtitle: A quiet evening in ninth-century Baghdad
subject: a baker in Abbasid Baghdad
synopsis: A gentle walk through the routines, sounds, and small obligations of a market baker as the city settles toward sleep.

chapters.title: Before the First Ember
chapters.summary: The baker wakes before dawn and prepares grain, water, and a quiet workbench.
chapters.transcript: Before the first ember brightens, the room is still and cool.

chapters.title: The Market Softens
chapters.summary: The stall closes slowly while neighbors trade small words and familiar goods.
chapters.transcript: By late afternoon, the market has grown softer around the edges.

sources.title: Daily Life in Abbasid Baghdad
sources.notes: General context source for Abbasid urban life.

sources.title: Bread and Urban Foodways
sources.notes: Placeholder fixture source for bread-making details.
